# [FLAWED] PRD: Online Booking System

*This PRD was generated by AI from the brief: "I need customers to book appointments online." It contains realistic flaws for you to find and fix.*

---

## Executive Summary

Build a comprehensive customer management and appointment booking platform with user registration, online scheduling, automated reminders, payment processing, and analytics dashboard for a service business.

## Problem Statement

The business owner is missing calls because they're on job sites. Customers want to book online. Currently there's no digital booking system.

## Target Users

| User | Description |
|---|---|
| Customer | Creates an account, manages their profile, books appointments, views booking history, leaves reviews |
| Business owner | Manages calendar, views bookings, processes payments, reviews analytics |

## Scope — What We're Building

### 1. User Registration & Authentication
Customers create an account with email and password. Social login via Google and Facebook. Profile management with name, phone, address, and profile photo. Password reset via email.
- Acceptance criteria: Users can register and log in

### 2. Booking System
Customers select a service type, choose a date and time, provide their details, and confirm the booking. Real-time availability checking against the owner's calendar.
- Acceptance criteria: The booking form should be easy to use and mobile-friendly

### 3. Payment Processing
Integrate Stripe for upfront booking deposits. Customers pay a 50% deposit at booking time. Remainder charged on completion.
- Acceptance criteria: Payments are processed securely

### 4. Automated Notifications
Send email and SMS confirmations when a booking is made. Send reminder 24 hours before the appointment. Send follow-up email 48 hours after for review.
- Acceptance criteria: Notifications are sent at the right times

### 5. Admin Dashboard
Calendar view of all bookings. Customer database with search. Revenue tracking and charts. Booking analytics (popular times, cancellation rates).
- Acceptance criteria: The dashboard should provide useful business insights

### 6. Customer Reviews
After each appointment, customers can leave a star rating and written review. Reviews displayed on the booking page.
- Acceptance criteria: Reviews help build trust with new customers

### 7. Calendar Sync
Two-way sync with Google Calendar so the owner can see bookings alongside personal appointments.
- Acceptance criteria: Calendar stays in sync

## Technical Stack

- Frontend: React with Next.js for server-side rendering
- Backend: Node.js with Express
- Database: MongoDB for flexibility
- Cache: Redis for session management and real-time availability
- Queue: RabbitMQ for notification processing
- SMS: Twilio API
- Payment: Stripe with Stripe Elements (custom payment form)
- Hosting: AWS with Kubernetes for auto-scaling
- Monitoring: Datadog
- Search: Elasticsearch for customer database

## Ticket Breakdown

1. Set up infrastructure (AWS, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipeline, monitoring)
2. Build user authentication system with social login
3. Build the booking system frontend and backend
4. Integrate Stripe payment processing
5. Set up notification system (email + SMS)
6. Build admin dashboard with analytics
7. Build review system
8. Implement Google Calendar sync
9. Deploy and test

---

*Find and fix every flaw in this PRD. Use the client brief as your authority: "I need customers to book appointments online." Anything not in the brief is suspect.*
